h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: The four-in-ten-thousand figure is still the bit that makes this feel slippery.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to proceed directly to discussing the 2 watts per square metre figure introduced by the tutor.

scored.recoheredEarlier: If the words are exact, it seems fair at first glance.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would simply try to identify which of the four listed elements was missing from the quote.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought the wall map was giving real size, but the atlas label undercuts that: Greenland is 2.2 million, Africa is 30 million.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was moving forward to verify the scale bar and refine the public presentation line.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Maybe I was letting the projected average become, almost without noticing, a statement about what one young visitor is probably like.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to tentatively adjust their phrasing of the survey results while struggling to define the boundary of the word 'probably'.

scored.recoheredEarlier: So the first visitor line is the trap: “Strong steel, safe bridge,” and then the truck proves that is too quick.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to proceed directly to practicing the physical sequence of the bridge components as prompted by the tutor.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: The leaflet pile helps a bit
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to refine their definition of antibiotic resistance by shifting focus from the patient to the bacteria.
